title: Server Pipeline Refactor
goal: Consolidate the overlapping auto-mode and Lead Engineer control planes into a clean Scheduler + Feature Pipeline architecture, eliminate the broadcast event storm, and make the system ready for Ava chat as the primary operator interface.

prd.situation: protoLabs Studio has two overlapping control planes: AutoModeService (5,007 lines) doing scheduling, execution, failure tracking, status management, and event routing; and LeadEngineerService with 15 companion files (~4,700 lines total) that wraps auto-mode and adds real value (PR lifecycle closure, plan generation, failure classification, 14 board rules). The delegation runs in circles: auto-mode → LE.process() → EXECUTE processor → auto-mode.executeFeature() → ExecutionService → callbacks back into auto-mode. The event bus has 263 types with 30+ services subscribing to ALL events and filtering internally — a broadcast storm. The product is shifting from a CLI operator (Ava skill) to a UI chat interface as the primary entry point, making the architecture seams visible and painful.
prd.problem: The circular delegation between auto-mode and LE causes repeated integration bugs (dep check inconsistency, circuit breaker not firing, cooldown race). The god-object auto-mode-service is untestable at the seams — ExecutionService has zero direct unit tests. The broadcast-all event bus means every event wakes 30+ services. There is no clean contract between the scheduler and the pipeline, making it impossible to route Ava chat requests through the same path as auto-mode without duplicating logic. Escalation and authority systems are currently flagged off, creating a window to refactor before they reactivate.
prd.approach: Establish a test foundation first, then refactor in four phases: (1) Give LE.process() a structured return type so the scheduler can interpret outcomes without relying on catch blocks. (2) Move status management, model selection, and event emission from auto-mode callbacks into LE pipeline processors — pipeline owns its state. (3) Extract a thin FeatureScheduler from auto-mode-service targeting ~500 lines. (4) Add typed filtered subscriptions to EventEmitter and migrate hot-path services off broadcast-all. Each phase is independently deployable and verified by the test suite established in M1.
prd.results: auto-mode-service shrinks from 5,007 to ~500-800 lines of pure scheduling logic. LE becomes THE single execution path with no fallback executeFeature branch. The Scheduler-Pipeline contract is a typed interface. Event subscriptions are filtered at the bus level. Ava chat and REST both route through the same Pipeline entry point. All refactored code has unit and integration test coverage.
constraints: No feature flags on Lead Engineer — it is always wired and always active. Refactor must be live-path.
All phases must keep the server running — no big-bang rewrites. Each milestone must deploy to staging cleanly.
The pipeline flag (PipelineOrchestrator/HITL gates) must remain off — do not couple this refactor to that system.
Preserve the 14 board rules in lead-engineer-rules.ts exactly — they are working correctly.
ExecutionService.runAgent() is the leaf-level agent executor — it stays. Only the callback/wiring layer above it changes.
No changes to the client-facing API surface (routes, MCP tools) during refactor phases.

researchSummary: IAutoModeCallbacks interface (execution-types.ts:73-129) is the exact scheduler-pipeline contract seam. ExecutionService has zero direct unit tests — tested only via auto-mode integration. LE is always-on (no feature flag). Two external executeFeature() callers: REST run-feature.ts and Ava chat ava-tools.ts. Circular delegation: auto-mode → LE.process() → ExecuteProcessor → auto-mode.executeFeature() → ExecutionService → callbacks back into auto-mode. Circuit breaker, failure tracking, model selection, status callbacks all live in auto-mode despite being pipeline concerns. Board rules (14, in lead-engineer-rules.ts) work correctly and should be preserved unchanged. pipeline featureFlag controls PipelineOrchestrator (HITL gates) not LE — keep off.

milestones.title: Test Foundation
milestones.description: Establish the regression safety net before touching any production code. Cover the exact seams that will be split: the scheduler loop, the pipeline state transitions, and the IAutoModeCallbacks contract.

phases.title: Scheduler unit tests
phases.description: Write unit tests for the auto-mode scheduling logic: loadPendingFeatures (dep resolution, unblocking, git-workflow guard), circuit breaker (cooldown, auto-resume), and concurrency lease management. Mock the Pipeline delegation point. Expand auto-mode-service.test.ts and add scheduler-loop.test.ts. These tests must pass before and after M3.
acceptanceCriteria: loadPendingFeatures: tests for dep-satisfied, dep-not-satisfied, foundation vs non-foundation, git-workflow block guard
Circuit breaker: pause fires after 3 failures in window, cooldown timer fires, auto-resume restarts loop
Concurrency: max concurrency respected, double-start race prevented
All tests pass with npm run test:server

phases.title: Pipeline state machine tests
phases.description: Write integration tests for the Lead Engineer pipeline covering all 7 states and key transition paths using AUTOMAKER_MOCK_AGENT=true. Cover: happy path (INTAKE→PLAN→EXECUTE→REVIEW→MERGE→DONE), ESCALATE from INTAKE (unmet deps), ESCALATE from EXECUTE (agent failure). Verify statusChangeReason and failureCount are set on ESCALATE.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: feature moves INTAKE→PLAN→EXECUTE→REVIEW→MERGE→DONE, status=done at end
INTAKE ESCALATE: unmet dep → blocked, statusChangeReason set, failureCount incremented
EXECUTE ESCALATE: mock agent failure → blocked after MAX_RETRIES
All tests pass with npm run test:server

phases.title: ExecutionService contract tests
phases.description: Write direct unit tests for ExecutionService (apps/server/src/services/auto-mode/execution-service.ts) — currently has zero tests. Focus on the IAutoModeCallbacks contract: each callback is called at the right time. Mock all callbacks. Test: agent start emits progress, completion calls updateFeatureStatus, failure calls trackFailureAndCheckPauseForProject, HITL gate calls waitForPlanApproval.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent start: emitAutoModeEvent called with feature:started
Agent success: updateFeatureStatus called with done, recordSuccessForProject called
Agent failure: trackFailureAndCheckPauseForProject called with error info
HITL gate: waitForPlanApproval called when plan approval enabled
All tests pass with npm run test:server

milestones.title: Structured Pipeline Results
milestones.description: Give LE.process() a typed return value so the scheduler can interpret outcomes without relying on catch blocks. Eliminates the class of bugs where ESCALATE returns success to the scheduler.

phases.title: PipelineResult type and LE return value
phases.description: Define PipelineResult in libs/types/src/lead-engineer.ts: { outcome: 'completed' | 'escalated' | 'blocked' | 'needs_retry'; finalState: LeadEngineerState; reason?: string; failureCount?: number; retryAfterMs?: number }. Update LeadEngineerService.process() to return PipelineResult instead of void. Surface the finalState already tracked at lead-engineer-service.ts:397. Update lead-engineer:feature-processed event payload to include outcome.
acceptanceCriteria: PipelineResult type exported from @protolabsai/types
leadEngineerService.process() returns PipelineResult
ESCALATE maps to outcome escalated
DONE maps to outcome completed
npm run typecheck passes, npm run build:packages passes

phases.title: Scheduler interprets PipelineResult
phases.description: Update the auto-mode dispatch block (auto-mode-service.ts:1172-1197) to switch on PipelineResult.outcome. On completed: call recordSuccessForProject. On escalated: call trackFailureAndCheckPauseForProject, trigger circuit breaker if threshold hit. On needs_retry: schedule retry with retryAfterMs. Remove the outer .catch() circuit-breaker workaround — the structured result replaces it.
acceptanceCriteria: outcome=completed: recordSuccessForProject called, startingFeatures cleared
outcome=escalated: trackFailureAndCheckPauseForProject called, circuit breaker can fire
outcome=needs_retry: retry scheduled with retryAfterMs delay
Outer .catch() circuit-breaker workaround removed
M1 pipeline integration tests still pass

milestones.title: Pipeline Owns Its State
milestones.description: Move model selection, status updates, and event emission from auto-mode callbacks into the pipeline processors. The pipeline owns its state machine — the scheduler only provides the feature ID.

phases.title: Move model selection into INTAKE
phases.description: Move auto-mode-service.getModelForFeature() (lines 426-471) into IntakeProcessor.process(). INTAKE selects the model and stores it in StateContext. Remove the getModelForFeature call from the auto-mode dispatch block (line 1160). Remove getModelForFeature from IAutoModeCallbacks. The as unknown as ExecuteOptions cast disappears.
acceptanceCriteria: IntakeProcessor selects model based on complexity and feature type
auto-mode dispatch block no longer calls getModelForFeature
as unknown as ExecuteOptions cast removed
npm run typecheck passes
M1 tests still pass

phases.title: Pipeline processors emit events directly
phases.description: Replace the emitAutoModeEvent callback in ExecutionService with direct event emission through the shared EventEmitter (available in processors via serviceContext.events). Each processor emits its own lifecycle events directly. Remove emitAutoModeEvent from IAutoModeCallbacks. Update ExecutionService to accept events directly instead of via callback.
acceptanceCriteria: emitAutoModeEvent removed from IAutoModeCallbacks
ExecutionService emits events directly via this.events
Pipeline processors emit phase-transition events directly
npm run typecheck passes, M1 tests pass

phases.title: Pipeline processors own status updates
phases.description: Remove updateFeatureStatus and updateFeaturePlanSpec from IAutoModeCallbacks. Each LE processor calls featureLoader.update() directly (ESCALATE already does this after our recent fix). Complete the pattern: EXECUTE updates status to in_progress on start and done on success; REVIEW updates to review on PR creation; MERGE updates to done on merge.
acceptanceCriteria: updateFeatureStatus and updateFeaturePlanSpec removed from IAutoModeCallbacks
All status updates go through featureLoader.update() in processors
ExecutionService no longer manages feature status
npm run typecheck passes, all M1 tests pass

milestones.title: Thin Scheduler Extraction
milestones.description: Extract the remaining scheduling logic from auto-mode-service.ts into a clean FeatureScheduler, then remove the legacy executeFeature fallback path.

phases.title: Extract FeatureScheduler class
phases.description: Create apps/server/src/services/feature-scheduler.ts. Move loadPendingFeatures(), resolveMaxConcurrency(), feature selection logic, and the core loop body into FeatureScheduler. FeatureScheduler takes a PipelineRunner interface: { run(projectPath, featureId): Promise<PipelineResult> }. AutoModeService implements PipelineRunner by delegating to leadEngineerService.process(). Clean inversion: Scheduler depends on interface, Pipeline implements it.
acceptanceCriteria: FeatureScheduler class with PipelineRunner interface
loadPendingFeatures, feature selection, concurrency management in FeatureScheduler
auto-mode-service delegates to FeatureScheduler
No logic duplication between the two files
npm run typecheck passes, all M1 tests pass

phases.title: Remove legacy executeFeature fallback
phases.description: Remove the ternary fallback in auto-mode dispatch (auto-mode-service.ts:1173-1183). Update run-feature.ts and ava-tools.ts to route through leadEngineerService.process() directly. Update setLeadEngineerService setter to throw if called with null — required now, not optional. Add startup guard in wiring.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: No ternary LE-or-fallback branch in auto-mode dispatch
run-feature.ts routes through leadEngineerService.process()
ava-tools.ts routes through leadEngineerService.process()
Startup throws if leadEngineerService not wired
npm run typecheck passes, all M1 and integration tests pass

milestones.title: Typed Event Bus
milestones.description: Add filtered typed subscriptions to EventEmitter and migrate hot-path services off broadcast-all. Backwards-compatible — existing subscribe() calls continue to work.

phases.title: Add typed on() method to EventEmitter
phases.description: Add on<T extends EventType>(type: T, handler: (payload: EventPayloadMap[T]) => void): UnsubscribeFn to EventEmitter interface and implementation in apps/server/src/lib/events.ts. Internally maintain a Map<EventType, Set<handler>> alongside the existing subscribers Set. Add EventPayloadMap type to libs/types/src/event.ts for core types: feature:completed, feature:status-changed, feature:error, auto-mode:event, lead-engineer:feature-processed.
acceptanceCriteria: bus.on('feature:completed', handler) compiles and fires only on that event type
Existing bus.subscribe(allEventsCallback) still works
EventPayloadMap covers 5 core event types
npm run typecheck passes, npm run build:packages passes

phases.title: Migrate auto-mode and LE to typed subscriptions
phases.description: Replace generic subscribe() calls in auto-mode-service.ts (line 337, feature:status-changed) and lead-engineer-service.ts (project:lifecycle:launched, lead-engineer:project-completing-requested) with typed on() subscriptions. These two services previously woke on all 263 event types.
acceptanceCriteria: auto-mode-service uses bus.on('feature:status-changed') instead of subscribe
lead-engineer-service uses typed on() for all its subscriptions
No broadcast-all subscribe() calls remain in these two files
npm run typecheck passes, all M1 tests pass

phases.title: Migrate escalation-router and notification-router
phases.description: Migrate escalation-router.ts and notification-router.ts from broadcast-all subscribe() to typed on() subscriptions — the highest-volume handlers outside auto-mode/LE. Document remaining broadcast-all services with a TODO comment.
acceptanceCriteria: escalation-router uses typed on() for escalation:* and feature:blocked events
notification-router uses typed on() for notification:created
Remaining broadcast-all services documented with TODO: migrate to bus.on()
npm run typecheck passes, all M1 tests pass
